## Overview

When your application calls the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1442535-aeputarray] function to put informationinto a descriptor list or the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1445720-aegetarray] functionto get information from a descriptor list, it uses an to store theinformation. The type of array depends on the data for the array,as specified by one of the constants described in [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/apple_events/1542848-data_array_constants].

Array items in Apple event arrays of type `kAEDataArray`, `kAEPackedArray`,or `kAEHandleArray` mustbe factored—that is, contained in a factored descriptor list.Before adding array items to a factored descriptor list, you shouldprovide both a pointer to the data that is common to all array itemsand the size of that common data when you first call [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1448643-aecreatelist] to createa factored descriptor list. When you call `AEPutArray` toadd the array data to such a descriptor list, the Apple Event Managerautomatically isolates the common data you specified in the callto `AECreateList`.

When you call `AEGetArray` or `AEPutArray`,you specify a pointer of data type `AEArrayDataPointer` thatpoints to a buffer containing the data for the array.

# AEArrayData

**Structure**

Stores array information to be put into a descriptor listwith the `AEPutArray` functionor extracted from a descriptor list with the `AEGetArray` function.

## Declaration

```swift
struct AEArrayData
```

## Overview

When your application calls the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1442535-aeputarray] function to put informationinto a descriptor list or the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1445720-aegetarray] functionto get information from a descriptor list, it uses an to store theinformation. The type of array depends on the data for the array,as specified by one of the constants described in [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/apple_events/1542848-data_array_constants].

Array items in Apple event arrays of type `kAEDataArray`, `kAEPackedArray`,or `kAEHandleArray` mustbe factored—that is, contained in a factored descriptor list.Before adding array items to a factored descriptor list, you shouldprovide both a pointer to the data that is common to all array itemsand the size of that common data when you first call [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/coreservices/1448643-aecreatelist] to createa factored descriptor list. When you call `AEPutArray` toadd the array data to such a descriptor list, the Apple Event Managerautomatically isolates the common data you specified in the callto `AECreateList`.

When you call `AEGetArray` or `AEPutArray`,you specify a pointer of data type `AEArrayDataPointer` thatpoints to a buffer containing the data for the array. 

## Instance Properties

- **kAEDataArray**
- **kAEDescArray**
- **kAEHandleArray**
- **kAEKeyDescArray**
- **kAEPackedArray**

## Initializers

- **init()**
- **init(kAEDataArray:)**
- **init(kAEDescArray:)**
- **init(kAEHandleArray:)**
- **init(kAEKeyDescArray:)**
- **init(kAEPackedArray:)**
